conclusions.fixed_order_five: every consecutive-row arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel minor through order five has its required sign on -100<=lambda<=0
conclusions.next_handoff: derive a cancellation-preserving contiguous order-six entry coordinate and flow reduction

proof_boundary: This artifact proves the required signed Hankel layer through fixed order five only. It does not prove order six or higher, PF-infinity, the all-degree Jensen bridge, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Every order-five endpoint coordinate is strictly positive at lambda=-100.
Order-five positivity is uniform at sufficiently large shift throughout the compact heat interval.
rows.formula: there exists N_5 such that H_(5,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=N_5 and -100<=lambda<=0

rows.claim: The normalized order-five layer obeys a nearest-neighbor cooperative system with positive forward coefficient.
The uniform tail confines any first loss to finitely many shifts, where variation of constants preserves positivity.
rows.proof_boundary: Infinite-index maximum principle with compact-uniform tail.

rows.claim: The endpoint, uniform tail, and cooperative system prove contiguous order five throughout the interval.
rows.proof_boundary: All shifts and all finite heat parameters from -100 through zero.

rows.claim: The completed lower contiguous layers supply every initial-minor sign through order four.
rows.formula: epsilon_k*H_(k,n)(lambda)>0 for k=1,2,3,4, all n and -100<=lambda<=0

rows.claim: The fixed-order initial-minor criterion transfers signed contiguous order five to all arbitrary column sets.
rows.proof_boundary: Finite-dimensional transfer applied pointwise in lambda.

rows.claim: The fixed-order programme now hands off to a genuinely new contiguous order-six layer.
rows.formula: derive a cancellation-preserving contiguous order-six entry coordinate and flow reduction
rows.proof_boundary: Open order-six analytic problem; not PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

source_contract.conditional_forward: [H_(5,n)(-100)>0 for all n] => [H_(5,n)(lambda)>0 for all n and -100<=lambda<=0]
source_contract.fixed_order_transfer: [epsilon_k H_(k,s)>0 for 1<=k<=m, all s] => [epsilon_k R_(k,n)(j_1,...,j_k)>0 for 1<=k<=m]
source_contract.lower_layers: epsilon_k*H_(k,n)(lambda)>0 for k=1,2,3,4, all n and -100<=lambda<=0
source_contract.uniform_tail: there exists N_5 such that H_(5,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=N_5 and -100<=lambda<=0

status: contiguous and arbitrary-column order-five theorem on -100<=lambda<=0
